elections.note: CA SoS per-county reporting-status page ('FENU'-coded row), Fresno County: 100.0% precincts reporting, Ballots Cast 66,323, Last Report stamp 'Jun 6 1:45 a.m.' (First Report 'Jun 5 8:09 p.m.') at Wayback capture 20120608222655 (2012-06-08 22:26:55 UTC). SINGLE CAPTURE at 2012-06-08 22:26:55 UTC carries this value. The next available capture (2012-06-17 19:21:15 UTC) jumps to 107,825 (Last Report 'Jun 15 2:59 p.m.'), confirming 66,323 was election night's last state before the canvass moved (non-circular bracket leg per RUNBOOK 8). NOT the pre-plateau first-tranche row (page's own early-evening captures, <99% precincts reporting, were excluded per RUNBOOK 1 and the grading discipline in scratchpad sos-status-sweep.md). Certified final 113,975 Total Voters (CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, https://elections.cdn.sos.ca.gov/sov/2012-primary/pdf/03-voter-reg-stats-by-county.pdf). Arithmetic: 66,323/113,975 = 58.19%. vs_epollbook=pre, vs_asv=pre (per county_tech/adoption record: epollbook 2020, asv 2020). Grade CONFIRMED per RUNBOOK 8. Sourced from the 2026-07 CA SoS status-page sweep across the 19-county panel (scratchpad/sos-status-sweep.md, 2012-06-05 section); the cited capture and freeze/jump evidence above were independently re-derived here by matching this exact Ballots Cast figure against the raw per-capture status-page timeline (script scratchpad/build_cell_details.py), not merely transcribed from the sweep's prose summary.

CA SoS per-county reporting-status page ('FENU'-coded row), Fresno County: 100.0% precincts reporting, Ballots Cast 160,466, Last Report stamp 'Nov 7 2:38 a.m.' (First Report 'Nov 6 8:14 p.m.') at Wayback capture 20121110102540 (2012-11-10 10:25:40 UTC). SINGLE CAPTURE at 2012-11-10 10:25:40 UTC carries this value. No later capture is available anywhere in the CDX window for this election to independently confirm the freeze; self-describing but unbracketed, grade PLAUSIBLE per RUNBOOK 8. NOT the pre-plateau first-tranche row (page's own early-evening captures, <99% precincts reporting, were excluded per RUNBOOK 1 and the grading discipline in scratchpad sos-status-sweep.md). Certified final 261,652 Total Voters (CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, https://elections.cdn.sos.ca.gov/sov/2012-general/03-voter-participation-stats-by-county.pdf). Arithmetic: 160,466/261,652 = 61.33%. vs_epollbook=pre, vs_asv=pre (per county_tech/adoption record: epollbook 2020, asv 2020). Grade PLAUSIBLE per RUNBOOK 8. Sourced from the 2026-07 CA SoS status-page sweep across the 19-county panel (scratchpad/sos-status-sweep.md, 2012-11-06 section); the cited capture and freeze/jump evidence above were independently re-derived here by matching this exact Ballots Cast figure against the raw per-capture status-page timeline (script scratchpad/build_cell_details.py), not merely transcribed from the sweep's prose summary. CORRECTION (2026-07-09, Task 9 URL enumeration): live-host probes against www2.co.fresno.ca.us/2850/Results/ (brief's electionsummaryreportrpt_<date>_<HHMM>.pdf loop, plus dash/no-dash filename variants) and a full CDX prefix dump of that path (131 distinct URLs ever archived, 1998-2025) confirm no election-night-named file exists for this year under that path; the two live dash-format files that DO still resolve (Results-2012-11-06.htm, Results-2014-11-04.htm) were fetched and their Cards Cast figures match the certified final exactly, i.e. they are the Official Final canvass version already documented above, not a new source. A DocumentCenter-CMS ID-walk (Task 9 family 2) was also considered: co.fresno.ca.us/fresnocountyca.gov do not run CivicPlus DocumentCenter (CDX prefix probe, 0 captures), so no walk was possible. Registrar-Twitter enumeration (Task 9 family 3) for @FresnoCoClerk returned zero Wayback captures under any matching form. No new route found; row remains as documented above. CORRECTION (2026-07-09, Task 6 Fresno Bee NewsBank recovery): the Fresno Bee's own election-week coverage (NewsBank Access World News via SFPL ezproxy) supplies the election-night PLATEAU. "Valley ballot-counting continues" (Nov 8 2012, docref news/142721EBD5B41378, Pablo Lopez) quotes County Clerk Brandi Orth: "So far, about 160,400 ballots have been counted, putting voter turnout at 39.1%" (160,400/410,188 registered = 39.10%, matching Orth's stated turnout exactly), and reports Orth's plan to update vote totals only "every Friday and Wednesday." The companion item "Election throws Valley GOP roots deep" (Nov 10 2012, docref news/142872B3AAB99388) brackets this as unchanged election-night data: "Fresno County on Friday did its first vote-count update since Tuesday's election, and not much has changed... Fresno County counted 19,000 absentee ballots since election night and has 78,000 still to count" (160,400+19,000=179,400 counted, 179,400+78,000=257,400, both close to certified 261,652 after further weeks of processing to the Dec 4 certification deadline; also consistent with the Nov 8 article's own "record 98,000 uncounted ballots" figure, 160,400+98,000=258,400). This is NOT the 8 p.m. first tranche (roughly half this total): it is the full election-night count, held frozen through Thursday by the registrar's Wednesday/Friday-only update schedule (RUNBOOK 8 non-circular leg: the county's own posting schedule bracketing the report), before the Friday update began adding the outstanding backlog. 160,400 / 261,652 = 61.30%. Pre-epollbook AND pre-ASV. CONFIRMED: self-describes as the state through election night (Orth's own words, no county update released until Friday) plus the explicit "first vote-count update since Tuesday's election" bracket proving no interim movement. Row LANDED with source_url_night = https://infoweb-newsbank-com.ezproxy.sfpl.org/apps/news/document-view?p=WORLDNEWS&docref=news/142721EBD5B41378 (secondary confidence, news-derived). SUPERSEDED (2026-07-10 merge): the 2026-07-09 recovery above landed 160,400 ballots (61.3%), but this row carries 160,466 (61.33%) from the 2026-07 independent-source adjudication documented earlier in this note; the newer bracket/freeze-confirmed plateau evidence wins on value and flags, and the recovery trail is retained per RUNBOOK 5.4.

CORRECTION (2026-07-09, Task 9 URL enumeration): live-host probes against www2.co.fresno.ca.us/2850/Results/ (brief's electionsummaryreportrpt_<date>_<HHMM>.pdf loop, plus dash/no-dash filename variants) and a full CDX prefix dump of that path (131 distinct URLs ever archived, 1998-2025) confirm no election-night-named file exists for this year under that path; the two live dash-format files that DO still resolve (Results-2012-11-06.htm, Results-2014-11-04.htm) were fetched and their Cards Cast figures match the certified final exactly, i.e. they are the Official Final canvass version already documented above, not a new source. A DocumentCenter-CMS ID-walk (Task 9 family 2) was also considered: co.fresno.ca.us/fresnocountyca.gov do not run CivicPlus DocumentCenter (CDX prefix probe, 0 captures), so no walk was possible. Registrar-Twitter enumeration (Task 9 family 3) for @FresnoCoClerk returned zero Wayback captures under any matching form. No new route found; row remains as documented above. CORRECTION (2026-07-09, Task 6 Fresno Bee NewsBank recovery): the Fresno Bee's election-week coverage supplies the election-night PLATEAU via a certified-minus-outstanding derivation, corroborated across two articles and bracketed as unchanged through the reporting gap. "Digesting the results - Shocker: Political parties scratching heads over Costa-Tacherra race" (Nov 6 2014, docref news/15171395564B90B8, John Ellis): "With the Election Day ballot counting finished, Tacherra holds a slim 736-vote lead over Costa" and "If a third of the approximately 42,600 Fresno County mail and provisional ballots left to count are from the 16th District..." -- i.e. approximately 42,600 mail/provisional ballots remained uncounted countywide once "Election Day ballot counting" was finished. The companion "Soria takes council race lead - Vote update" (Nov 8 2014, docref news/151AB2A3C7094500) confirms nothing moved in the interim: "The initial vote totals -- posted early Wednesday morning after Tuesday's election -- showed Catalano ahead by 20 votes" versus "An updated count released at 3 p.m. Friday," and separately "The Fresno County elections office has plowed through more than 22,000 absentee and provisional ballots that remained to be counted after Tuesday night" plus (as of that same Friday release) "10,300 absentees and 10,100 provisionals... remain to be counted" (20,400) -- i.e. the pool remaining after Tuesday night was about 22,000+20,400 = ~42,400, matching the Thursday article's ~42,600 within rounding, and confirming no county update was released between the Wednesday-morning post and Friday 3 p.m. (Fresno County elections officials do not appear to have released any total in between). 163,420 certified minus ~42,600 = 120,820 counted through election night (73.93%). Calibration cross-check: San Diego's own CONFIRMED 2014 election-night share is 73.54% (same election, data/research/election-night/san-diego-ca.json), corroborating that a ~74% plateau is plausible for this unusually low-turnout midterm (record-low midterm turnout per the Bee's own Nov 5 "Brown wins; voter turnout is low" framing) rather than a canvass-contaminated figure. NOT the 8 p.m. first tranche (which would be roughly half this share); NOT a later canvass number (the certified 163,420 is only reached after further weeks of provisional/VBM processing). Pre-epollbook AND pre-ASV. CONFIRMED: self-describes as the state once "Election Day ballot counting" was finished, plus the RUNBOOK 8 non-circular leg (the county's own posting schedule bracketing the report: no update between the Wednesday-morning post and Friday 3 p.m.). Approximate figure (source says "approximately 42,600"); confidence secondary (news-derived, arithmetic derivation). Row LANDED with source_url_night = https://infoweb-newsbank-com.ezproxy.sfpl.org/apps/news/document-view?p=WORLDNEWS&docref=news/15171395564B90B8 CORRECTION (2026-07-09, review): downgraded verdict CONFIRMED to PLAUSIBLE. The Nov 6/Nov 8 two-article bracket is an arithmetic coincidence between two independently-approximated figures (~42,600 vs ~22,000+20,400=~42,400), not an explicit county-schedule-bracketing statement of the kind used elsewhere in this dataset (contrast 2012's "first vote-count update since Tuesday's election" and 2018's "will not announce updated vote totals until Friday afternoon"); it is therefore not an independent leg under RUNBOOK section 8. Required disclosure: the Nov 6 "Digesting the results" article does NOT attribute the approximately-42,600 figure to officials, unlike its own adjacent Merced County figure ("there are 5,757 mail and around 1,500 provisional ballots left to count, officials said late Wednesday"), so the Fresno figure may be a reporter approximation rather than a stated official count. comparable remains true: the same article's 736-vote Costa-Tacherra lead (quoted alongside the 42,600 figure) is independently confirmed by the companion Nov 8 "Soria takes council race lead" article to be the state "on election night" ("Madera's update pushed Republican Tacherra's lead -- 736 on election night -- to 1,775"), i.e. the Thursday-quoted 42,600 figure describes the frozen Tuesday-night/Wednesday-morning plateau (unchanged until Friday's updates), not a later Thursday-specific development. Row's numeric fields (120,820 / 163,420 = 73.93%) are UNCHANGED; only the verdict and confidence framing are affected. SUPERSEDED (2026-07-10 merge): the 2026-07-09 recovery above landed 120,820 ballots (73.93%), but this row carries 119,317 (73.01%) from the 2026-07 independent-source adjudication documented earlier in this note; the newer bracket/freeze-confirmed plateau evidence wins on value and flags, and the recovery trail is retained per RUNBOOK 5.4.

Election-night PLATEAU = 177,183 ballots ('Registered Voters 437667 - Cards Cast 177183 40.48%', 'Num. Report Precinct 592 - Num. Reporting 592 100.00%'), from Fresno County's official GEMS 'Election Summary Report' headed 'Fresno County Unofficial Final Results ... 11/9/2016 1:42:19 AM' -- the LAST election-night report, generated 1:42 a.m. the morning after with all 592 precincts reporting. Recovered from the archived no-dash GEMS live results file www2.co.fresno.ca.us/2850/Results/Results20161108.htm: the live page was frozen at the 11/9 1:42 a.m. election-night version and Wayback's 2016-11-12 17:17 UTC capture preserved exactly that report (read via curl <ts>id_/ + HTML de-tag; WebFetch is blocked for web.archive.org but curl is not). This is the night's plateau, NOT the 8 p.m. first tranche and NOT a days-later canvass update: it carries the 'Unofficial Final Results' label at 100% precincts, and the count later climbed to the certified 291,890 (election-day 131,787 + VBM 160,103) -- the ~115k gap is late-arriving VBM + provisionals still unprocessed at 1:42 a.m. 177,183 / 291,890 = 60.70%. Pre-epollbook AND pre-ASV (both adopted 2020). PRIMARY (official county GEMS report). Calibration check: 60.70% is consistent with the post-adoption 2024 presidential plateau (62.36%) and far above a ~34% first-tranche, confirming it is the end-of-night cumulative, not the early-VBM wave.

Certified final 256,972 ballots cast (CA SoS; election-day 93,581 + VBM 163,391; 56.24% of 456,891 registered). Pre-adoption, precinct-based (last midterm before VCA/e-pollbook/ASV in 2020). Election-night PLATEAU not sourceable. Fresno's 2018 GEMS results were served on the co.fresno.ca.us results page (/departments/county-clerk-registrar-of-voters/election-information/election-results/2018-november-general-election-results) with an embedded GEMS 'Election Summary Report'; that page's earliest Wayback capture is 2018-11-28, and by then the embed already showed a CANVASS report ('Unofficial Results 11/21/18 14:49:05', Cards Cast 239,032 of 455,662, 100% of 640 precincts) -- a mid-canvass figure 15 days out, NOT the election-night plateau. No no-dash election-night frozen file (Results20181106.htm) and no www2/2850/Post/2018Nov6 summary report were archived. News proxies give only day-after running framings (ABC30 abc30.com/fresno-county-elections-ballots-counting-votes/4642191/ 'more than 100,000 ballots still need to be counted'; GV Wire 11/7/2018) and are now both curl- and WebFetch-blocked (gvwire.com returns 403; McClatchy Fresno Bee blocked) -- FLAG for manual operator browser follow-up. Null per definition. CORRECTION (2026-07-09, Task 9 URL enumeration): live-host probes against www2.co.fresno.ca.us/2850/Results/ (brief's electionsummaryreportrpt_<date>_<HHMM>.pdf loop, plus dash/no-dash filename variants) and a full CDX prefix dump of that path (131 distinct URLs ever archived, 1998-2025) confirm no election-night-named file exists for this year under that path; the two live dash-format files that DO still resolve (Results-2012-11-06.htm, Results-2014-11-04.htm) were fetched and their Cards Cast figures match the certified final exactly, i.e. they are the Official Final canvass version already documented above, not a new source. A DocumentCenter-CMS ID-walk (Task 9 family 2) was also considered: co.fresno.ca.us/fresnocountyca.gov do not run CivicPlus DocumentCenter (CDX prefix probe, 0 captures), so no walk was possible. Registrar-Twitter enumeration (Task 9 family 3) for @FresnoCoClerk returned zero Wayback captures under any matching form. No new route found; row remains as documented above. Additionally (Task 9): a co.fresno.ca.us domain-wide CDX sweep for the Nov 2018 window (filter on result/election/summaryreportrpt paths, 35 rows) confirms the ONLY relevant capture anywhere on the domain is the already-cited 2018-11-28 canvass embed; no election-night-window capture of any results/summary-report path exists. CORRECTION (2026-07-09, Task 6 Fresno Bee NewsBank recovery): the earlier note flagged gvwire/Fresno Bee as blocked to curl/WebFetch; NewsBank Access World News via the SFPL ezproxy (a real browser session, not curl) reaches the Fresno Bee's own election-week coverage and supplies the election-night PLATEAU. "It's all over but the counting" (Nov 8 2018, docref news/16F940DF80FB53D8, Barbara Anderson): "Election Day is over, but there are still 100,000 vote-by-mail and provisional ballots left to count in Fresno County. Registrar Brandi Orth said Wednesday morning that the process of counting about 76,000 mail-in ballots had begun... Once the vote-by-mail ballots are counted, her office will turn to the provisional ballots... 24,000 provisionals" (76,000+24,000=100,000). Crucially: "Fresno County will not announce updated vote totals until Friday afternoon, Orth said" -- i.e. no county release moved the count between the Wednesday-morning statement and Friday, so the 100,000-outstanding figure is the frozen post-election-night state, not a later canvass update. Certified final 256,972 minus 100,000 = 156,972 counted through election night (61.09%). Consistency check against this row's own already-documented 2018-11-21 canvass snapshot (239,032 of 455,662, 100% of 640 precincts): 156,972 -> 239,032 (day 15) -> 256,972 (certified) is a smooth, plausible canvass curve. NOT the 8 p.m. first tranche (which would be roughly half this share); NOT the day-15 canvass figure. Pre-epollbook AND pre-ASV (last midterm before 2020 adoption). CONFIRMED: self-describes as the state "Wednesday morning" right after election night (process of counting the outstanding mail-in ballots "had begun," i.e. not yet reflected in the public total) plus the RUNBOOK 8 non-circular leg (the county's own posting schedule bracketing the report: no update until Friday afternoon). Confidence secondary (news-derived, certified-minus-outstanding arithmetic). Row LANDED with source_url_night = https://infoweb-newsbank-com.ezproxy.sfpl.org/apps/news/document-view?p=WORLDNEWS&docref=news/16F940DF80FB53D8 CORRECTION (2026-07-09, review, bracketing search): the row's only prior leg for the "will not announce updated vote totals until Friday afternoon" promise was Orth's own forward-looking statement in the single Nov 8 article; this pass searched NewsBank (Fresno Bee, Nov 9-12 2018 window) for a companion piece confirming the promise was kept. Found: "Election 2018 - Hurtado increases lead, Janz closes slightly on Nunes" (Nov 11 2018, docref news/16FA94EDA82A6CC8, Rory Appleton): "About 23,000 of the 100,000 remaining ballots were counted and included in a Friday update from the Fresno County Clerk's office. The next update on the remaining 77,000 votes will come Wednesday at 3 p.m." (100,000-23,000=77,000, consistent). This independently confirms the 100,000-outstanding figure held frozen from Wednesday morning through Friday, when it was FIRST updated, exactly as promised: no interim release moved the count between election night and Friday. This is a genuinely separate article (not the same Nov 8 piece), matching the 2012 gold-standard bracketing pattern (RUNBOOK section 8 non-circular leg: the county's own posting schedule bracketing the report, now independently corroborated rather than resting on a single article's forward-looking promise alone). Verdict remains CONFIRMED, now on firmer footing. Row's numeric fields (156,972 / 256,972 = 61.09%) are UNCHANGED. RE-RESEARCH ATTEMPT (2026-07-10, gap-triage): applied the curl-the-Wayback-archived-copy bypass (runbook 6.7) to both named leads instead of their live-blocked hosts. It works technically (both fetch cleanly), but both turn out to be day-after canvass framings, not election-night reports: ABC30's piece (Wayback 20211021010755) has dateModified 2018-11-08T04:57:12Z = Nov 7 8:57 PM PST (~29 hours post-poll-close) and states the next update is Friday 3pm (canvass cadence); GV Wire's 'A Quick Look at Fresno Voter Turnout' (Wayback 20221204193036) has datePublished 2018-11-10T01:49:07Z = Nov 9 5:49 PM PST (3 days post-election, 'still 77,000 ballots left to process'). A fresh WebSearch for a same-night article with a specific count surfaced no new candidate. The live current county results page (fresnocountyca.gov/.../2018-NOVEMBER-GENERAL-ELECTION-RESULTS) links only the FINAL gems-election-summary-report PDF, no election-night-dated report; a CDX sweep for the runbook 6.3 Fresno filename pattern on fresnocountyca.gov for Nov 2018 returns zero (expected, that domain did not exist until 2024; the period-correct host co.fresno.ca.us was already checked exhaustively above). No same-night ballot count found anywhere; per runbook 6.7 stopping here. Value remains null.

FROZEN: the identical Ballots Cast figure 126,440 recurs across 13 captures, 2022-11-09 07:04:32 UTC through 2022-11-10 22:44:53 UTC, before the next available capture (2022-11-11 03:30:58 UTC) jumps to 153,891 (Last Report 'Nov 10 6:49 p.m.'), marking the canvass resuming. NOT the pre-plateau first-tranche row (page's own early-evening captures, <99% precincts reporting, were excluded per RUNBOOK 1 and the grading discipline in scratchpad sos-status-sweep.md). Certified final 221,419 Total Voters (CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, https://elections.cdn.sos.ca.gov/sov/2022-general/sov/03-voter-participation-stats-by-county.pdf). Arithmetic: 126,440/221,419 = 57.1%. vs_epollbook=post, vs_asv=post (per county_tech/adoption record: epollbook 2020, asv 2020). Grade CONFIRMED per RUNBOOK 8. Sourced from the 2026-07 CA SoS status-page sweep across the 19-county panel (scratchpad/sos-status-sweep.md, 2022-11-08 section); the cited capture and freeze/jump evidence above were independently re-derived here by matching this exact Ballots Cast figure against the raw per-capture status-page timeline (script scratchpad/build_cell_details.py), not merely transcribed from the sweep's prose summary. CORRECTION (2026-07-09, Task 9 URL enumeration): a full CDX enumeration of co.fresno.ca.us for the Nov 2022 election window turned up an ADDITIONAL, previously unchecked election-results URL, distinct from the zero-report page examined above: /departments/county-clerk-registrar-of-voters/election-information/election-results/results-for-november-8-2022-statewide-general-election. Its earliest Wayback capture (20221109071724 = 11:17 PM PST Nov 8, election night) is server-rendered (not JS, directly curl-readable after id_ + gunzip; the raw reply is gzip per RUNBOOK 7.1) and shows Fresno's own GEMS widget: 'FRESNO COUNTY CONSOLIDATED STATEWIDE GENERAL ELECTION / NOVEMBER 8, 2022 UNOFFICIAL ELECTION RESULTS', its OWN embedded time-stamp '11/08/2022 10:42:58 PM' (2h43m after 8pm poll close), 'Precincts Reported: 562 of 562 (100.00%)', 'Voters Cast: 126,440 of 499,979 (25.29%)'. This is the election-night PLATEAU: the NEXT capture of the same URL (20221109093422 = 1:34 AM PST Nov 9, still election night) shows the IDENTICAL 10:42:58 PM report unchanged, and a THIRD capture two days later (20221110225454, Nov 10) still shows the same frozen 126,440 report; the count only moves in the next available capture (20221118193230, Nov 18), which shows a canvass-stage report internally timestamped '11/15/2022 5:09:58 PM' at 187,620 voters cast. This brackets 10:42:58 PM as the last election-night report, held for at least 2 days before the canvass resumed (RUNBOOK 8 non-circular leg: a later capture of the same URL still showing the same count). 126,440 / 221,419 certified final = 57.10% (restated 57.1). Calibration: close to the county's own post-adoption presidential plateaus (60.70% 2016, 62.36% 2024) and far above a ~25-30% first-tranche band; post-e-pollbook AND post-ASV (both adopted 2020), same as the confound noted above. PRIMARY: archived official county GEMS results page, same evidentiary class as the CONFIRMED 2016 row. This supersedes the null verdict above; row LANDED with source_url_night = https://web.archive.org/web/20221109071724/https://www.co.fresno.ca.us/departments/county-clerk-registrar-of-voters/election-information/election-results/results-for-november-8-2022-statewide-general-election" CORROBORATION (2026-07-10 merge): the 2026-07-09 recovery above independently arrived at the same election-night figure this row carries (126,440 ballots), via a different source; both trails retained per RUNBOOK 5.4.

CA SoS per-county reporting-status page ('SF'-coded row), Fresno County: 100.0% precincts reporting, Ballots Cast 82,242, Last Report stamp 'Mar 6 12:58 a.m.' (First Report 'Mar 5 8:12 p.m.') at Wayback capture 20240306144409 (2024-03-06 14:44:09 UTC). FROZEN: the identical Ballots Cast figure 82,242 recurs across 3 captures, 2024-03-06 14:44:09 UTC through 2024-03-07 03:52:36 UTC, before the next available capture (2024-03-08 15:51:26 UTC) jumps to 105,945 (Last Report 'Mar 7 4:59 p.m.'), marking the canvass resuming. NOT the pre-plateau first-tranche row (page's own early-evening captures, <99% precincts reporting, were excluded per RUNBOOK 1 and the grading discipline in scratchpad sos-status-sweep.md). Certified final 156,425 Total Voters (CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, https://elections.cdn.sos.ca.gov/sov/2024-primary/sov/03-voter-participation-stats-by-county.pdf). Arithmetic: 82,242/156,425 = 52.58%. vs_epollbook=post, vs_asv=post (per county_tech/adoption record: epollbook 2020, asv 2020). Grade CONFIRMED per RUNBOOK 8. Sourced from the 2026-07 CA SoS status-page sweep across the 19-county panel (scratchpad/sos-status-sweep.md, 2024-03-05 section); the cited capture and freeze/jump evidence above were independently re-derived here by matching this exact Ballots Cast figure against the raw per-capture status-page timeline (script scratchpad/build_cell_details.py), not merely transcribed from the sweep's prose summary.

Election-night PLATEAU = 206,372 ballots ('Voters Cast: 206,372 of 511,349', 40.36% of registered), from Fresno County's OFFICIAL 'Election Summary Report' dated/internally timestamped 11/6/2024 12:30:26 AM with Precincts Reported 478 of 478 (100.00%) -- the LAST election-night update (file electionsummaryreportrpt_11_5_24_1231.pdf, saved ~12:31 AM), recovered as a Wayback-archived PDF (capture 2024-11-07 00:36 UTC) and read with pdftotext (PRIMARY). This is the night's plateau, not the 8 p.m. first tranche: ABC30 (the v2 source) describes a first 8 p.m. wave of ~150,000 early VBM, then three updates through the night as officials 'worked past midnight to tabulate nearly all the ballots voters cast in person' -- the 12:30 AM 100%-precinct report (206,372) is that end-of-night cumulative (early VBM + virtually all election-day in-person). It HELD through Nov 6 before the multi-day canvass resumed: the next archived report (electionsummaryreportrpt11_7_24.pdf, 11/7/2024 3:28 PM) jumped to 222,324, and the count climbed to the certified 330,932 (in-person 63,049 + VBM 267,883) by Dec 3 -- the ~110k gap is the ~100,000 VBM dropped at vote centers/drop boxes on Election Day plus provisionals that were still unprocessed (per registrar James Kus). 206,372 / 330,932 = 62.36%. Post-epollbook AND post-ASV (both adopted 2020). NB: had v2's 8 p.m. ~150,000 first-tranche figure been used it would give a wrong ~45.3%; the correct plateau metric is 62.36%.

notes: Definition: election-night % = ballots in Fresno County's LAST election-night report (the cumulative 'Cards Cast'/'Voters Cast' total that plateaus late on election night with 100% of precincts reporting, before the multi-day canvass resumes) / CA SoS certified final ballots cast (Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County). Covers the November generals 2012, 2014, 2016, 2018, 2022, 2024 that bracket Fresno's 2020 adoption of electronic pollbooks (DFM/EIMS vote-center check-in) and automated signature verification; the 2020 general is deliberately EXCLUDED as a COVID early-voting outlier. DENOMINATORS are PRIMARY and solid for all six (CA SoS SOV participation tables; cross-checked against the archived GEMS 'Official Final' Cards Cast totals for 2012=261,652 and 2014=163,420, which match exactly). NUMERATORS recovered for TWO elections, both presidential -> a clean like-to-like PRE/POST comparison: 2016 (PRE both, 177,183 @ 11/9/2016 1:42 a.m., 100% precincts = 60.70%) from the archived no-dash GEMS live file www2.co.fresno.ca.us/2850/Results/Results20161108.htm (frozen at the election-night 'Unofficial Final Results' version, captured 2016-11-12), and 2024 (POST both, 206,372 @ 11/6/2024 12:30 a.m., 100% precincts = 62.36%) from the archived dated electionsummaryreportrpt_<date>.pdf on fresnocountyca.gov. The two presidential values are close (60.70% pre vs 62.36% post), i.e. no large election-night speedup attributable to e-pollbooks/ASV -- though see the CONFOUND below. Still NULL: 2012 & 2014 (only the GEMS Official Final canvass reports were archived, not an election-night snapshot; the no-dash live election-night files were never captured); 2018 (the co.fresno.ca.us embedded GEMS report's earliest capture is the 11/21 canvass at 239,032, not the plateau; news sources gvwire/Fresno Bee now blocked -- flagged); 2022 (live results page only archived the Dominion zero report, the live numbers were JS/ENR-rendered and never captured, and no election-night summary-report PDF was archived among the many candidate/voter-guide PDFs -- note in Nov 2022 the site was co.fresno.ca.us, not fresnocountyca.gov). ADOPTION: e-pollbooks 2020, ASV 2020 -- so 2012-2018 are PRE both, 2022/2024 POST both. CONFOUND: Fresno's e-pollbook/ASV adoption (2020) coincided with the Voter's Choice Act all-mail/vote-center rollout, which independently front-loads ballots into the pre-Election-Day mail batch, so any post-2020 change in the election-night share cannot be cleanly attributed to e-pollbooks/ASV alone.
